                            Weapons | Expedition 33 Wiki


                            Weapons in Clair Obscur Expedition 33 are offensive tools that are essential to combat. Each weapon corresponds to various elemental affinities, which change the type of damage inflicted on enemies when using a basic attack or skills that use the weapon's element. These elements are Physical, Light, Dark, Void, Fire, Ice, Lightning, and Earth. Weapons scale in Attack Power, which is governed by improving the attribute level of Might, and most weapons have two or one corresponding attributes that scale based on the weapon level and can be further improved by improving the level of the corresponding attribute of the weapon and by equipping Pictos.

                            Weapons can be obtained in traditional ways, such as loot found in various locations, inside treasure chests known as Paint Cages, rewarded by NPCs for completing their requests, purchased from Gestral Merchants, acquired from defeating bosses, and specific enemies can also drop weapons after defeating them in the initial encounter.

                            On the other hand, some unique, specific boss and enemy encounters can provide free weapon upgrades if you already have the weapon in your inventory. Please check our Locations page to find complete location walkthroughs, or visit our Interactive Map page to discover all enemy and boss encounters in The Continent that initially provide weapon drops or upgrades. This page covers a list of all Weapons for all the Characters in Clair Obscur: Expedition 33.

                            • Please visit our Interactive Map to find all the available weapon drops or upgrades from specific enemy and boss encounters.
                            • Please visit our Paint Cages and Paint Spikes page to find secret areas or weapons that can be acquired from Paint Cages.

                             

                            Clair Obscur Expedition 33 All Weapons Guide

                            All Items
                            Weapons
                            Pictos
                            Quest Items
                            Tints
                            Music Records

                             

                            How to Upgrade Weapons in Expedition 33

                            Upgrading weapons in Clair Obscur Expedition 33 features a unique mechanic. There are two ways that a weapon can be upgraded to a higher level.

                            1. Some specific enemy or boss encounters will reward the player with weapon upgrades to specific weapons, provided that you have already acquired the weapon. These specific enemy encounters can be within Locations or on The Continent. These upgrades are only rewarded once or after winning the initial battle encounter.
                            2. The traditional way of upgrading weapons is by interacting with The Curator at Camp. As you progress through the game, you can use any Expedition Flag to upgrade weapons.

                            Upgrading Weapons and Resources requires Tints (or Materials), these can be acquired in various ways, such as loot, occasional random drops from defeating enemies, unique (one-time) drops from defeating Bosses or special enemies that do not respawn, purchased from Gestral Merchants, or acquired as reward for completing side quests.

                            For Upgrading Weapons, the following Tints are required:

                            • Chroma Catalyst: Can be used at Camp to upgrade weapons up to level 3.
                            • Polished Chroma Catalyst: Can be used at Camp to upgrade weapons up to level 9.
                            • Resplendent Chroma Catalyst: Can be used at Camp to upgrade weapons up to level 19.
                            • Grandiose Chroma Catalyst: Can be used at Camp to upgrade weapons up to level 32.
                            • Perfect Chroma Catalyst: Can be used at Camp to upgrade weapons up to level 33.

                            Clair Obscur Expedition 33 Beginner Guide

                            • Lvl. 10: Balanced Mask applies Powerful for 3 turns.
                            • Lvl: 20: Critical hits deal 30% more damage while in Balanced Mask.

                            Nusaro                            		 Dark 4197

                            Vitality
                            A

                            Agility
                            S
                            • Lvl. 4: Parries increase the Bestial Wheel by 1. Taking damage resets the Bestial Wheel.
                            • Lvl. 10: Upgraded Skills deal 30% more damage.
                            • Lvl: 20: +1 AP on Mask change.

                            Sidaro                            		 Dark 3035

                            Might
                            A

                            Luck
                            S
                            • Lvl. 4: 30% increased damage per Upgraded Skill used. Resets upon using a non-Upgraded Skill.
                            • Lvl. 10: Base Attack spins the Bestial Wheel to a random value.
                            • Lvl: 20: Using an Upgraded Skill gives 1 AP to all other allies.

                            Urnaro                            		 Earth 3422

                            Vitality
                            S

                            Luck
                            A
                            • Lvl. 4: Switch to Almighty Mask on Breaking an enemy.
                            • Lvl. 10: Almighty Mask gives 2 AP to all Allies.
                            • Lvl: 20: 50% increased Break damage.

                            Baguettaro                            		 Fire 3745

                            Defense
                            B

                            Luck
                            A

                            		  
                            • Lvl. 4: Critical hits apply Burn.
                            • Lvl. 10: Almighty Mask gives 50% increased damage (cumulative) and doubles enemies' Burn
                            • Lvl: 20: Almighty Mask Skills consume up to 100 Burn to deal 2% more damage per Burn consumed.

                            Esquiaro                            		  Earth 3745

                            Vitality
                            A

                            Defense
                            B
                            • Lvl. 4: Base Attack moves the Bestial Wheel to Heavy Mask.
                            • Lvl. 10: Heavy Mask gives 1 Shield to all allies.
                            • Lvl: 20: Damage taken are reduced by 50% while in Heavy Mask.


                            Cannaro

                            		 Light 3648

                            Agility
                            A

                            Luck
                            B
                            • Lvl. 4: Free Aim Shots move the Bestial Wheel 1 step forward.
                            • Lvl. 10: 25% increased damage per Mask change this turn.
                            • Lvl: 20: Free Aim shoots deal double damage while in Almighty Mask.

                                              Does anyone know if the names of the weapons are different depending on the language the player is using? I had a couple comments from Italian players on a YouTube video I made about getting Scaverim. They're saying that they're getting a different weapon.                                               • Anonymous
                                                23 Jun 2025 22:21  

                                                Just my two cents for the best few weapons for each character, feel free to voice your opinions:

                                                Gustave - Lanceram I guess.

                                                Verso - S tier: Chevalam, Contorso, Corpeso, Simoso, and Seeram (for spamming End Bringer cheese strats. It's pretty awful if not doing that.)
                                                A tier: Blodam, Confuso.
                                                Honorable mentions: Sireso is BUGGED as of 23 June 2025 and does not work with Powerful, which is the entire point of the weapon and is thus F- tier. If this is ever fixed, it will be easily S tier and maybe need to make an S+ tier just for that. Dualiso is S tier in Act II but falls off quite hard in Act III.

                                                Lune - Kralim is my endgame pick for rapid Elemental Genesis every other turn plus bonus AP generation is nice. This is for DPS builds. If you would rather rotate through the elements instead of spamming Elemental Genesis, Betelim is great for that.
                                                For support Lune, Benisim is really good as it works with Typhoon. Honorable mentions to Scaverim and Colim; both are good weapons and it wouldn't be wrong to build around them, I just don't like dark stains and the RNG nature of them both.

                                                Maelle - I think Medalum and Yeverum are equally amazing with Cheater. Medalum is strictly better if not using Cheater. Yeverum can generate massive amounts of shields, which can either save your ass if not great at parrying, or becomes one of the best offensive buffs in the game using Powerful Shield and Shield Affinity. If you are REALLY good at parrying, Jarum is also quite strong.

                                                Sciel - Martenon is still BUGGED as of 23 June 2025. When you acquire both Sun and Moon, if a teammate acts in between that occurring and Sciel's first Twilight turn, then Martenon's 4 and 10 abilities do not proc.
                                                With that said, if using support Sciel, Litheson is god tier and maybe the best weapon in the game. You will have way more turns between enemy actions than you are used to. For DPS Sciel, Chation is my personal favorite for the easy Foretell stacks. Charnon, Corderon, Bourgelon, and Guleson are all good choices also.

                                                Monoco - Joyaro and Urnaro are the clear winners for me. Starting in Almighty mask is insanely good, it makes anything he wants to do on turn 1 devastating. If Monoco is your main breaker, Urnaro's 3 abilities are all extremely good as well.

                                                +1

                                                4

                                                -1
                                                • Anonymous
                                                  02 Jun 2025 01:43  

                                                  NG+ Spoiler:

                                                  There is a Baguette weapon for each character exclusive to NG+
                                                  If you save the festival tokens from the prologue, you can spend them in the NG+ prologue at the bakery right after talking to Sophie. Which character you talk to the bakery with is the character that will receive the weapon.

                                                  ReplyReplies (1)

                                                  30

                                                  +1

                                                  4

                                                  -1
                                                  • Anonymous
                                                    31 May 2025 20:23  

                                                    18 May anon OP here, now in NG++. Since the way you play drastically changes once you hit over 500 lumina points on your characters (namely free aim spam w buffs and AP generation becomes cracked), here's my updated picks:

                                                    Gustave - No changes. Sorry, buddy.

                                                    Lune - Braselim. While it doesn't provide you with free stains or additional AP, the gradient charge generation is absolutely insane and lets you continuously abuse Sky Break (by far her best skill) and other characters' level 3s.

                                                    Maelle - Still Medalum. Starting in virtuose stance trumps everything else, as the 200% damage bonus from the get go is too good to pass up and it makes her that much more impactful from turn 1.

                                                    Sciel - Charnon. Being able to apply foretell with free aim completely changes the way you play her and elevates her to true godhood. Outside of twilight you can max out your stacks by shooting while only focusing on support with the standard Fortune's Fury/Intervention loop, then reap all the benefits in twilight with her nukes. Did you think she was broken before? You haven't seen anything yet.

                                                    Verso - Simoso. Nothing comes close.

                                                    Monoco - Fragaro. Probably the biggest surprise since I never even considered it before leaning into a free aim heavy playstyle. The gameplan is simple: roll for almighty mask, click Lampmaster Light. Every single turn.

                                                    Keep in mind that these recommendations only apply to NG+ onwards if you aim to break the game as much as you can with hyper-optimized builds. For your first run I still believe my earlier suggestions hold value. Enjoy the game guys!

                                                        Benisim + Revitalization + Typhoon + Picto for +2AP on healed allies + Cheater picto + 2ap/TURN picto + start with extra 2ap picto = 9AP per allies every turn and Lune who cast Genese every 3 turns. (So every 2 turn with cheater..)
                                                        With that you have all pictos who give defenseup, powerup, speedup on healed allies.
                                                        Dont need to parry, only dodge bcauz of this insane AP management.

                                                          • Anonymous
                                                            18 May 2025 16:27  

                                                            My own favorite picks for each character:

                                                            Gustave - Indifferent, just keep replacing them depending on which one scales best since they have no passives. Don't waste upgrades on them.

                                                            Lune - Trebuchim is fine until you can upgrade to level 10, at which point you can switch to Kralim or Betelim: they provide free stains on turn start and the damage bonus can be easily mantained with 100% uptime. Scaverim (which shares the same VIT/AGI scaling), might be stronger on paper, but dark stains feel clunky to play around with, so my endgame pick is still Kralim.

                                                            Maelle - Medalum is all you need (even after the nerf) and you can get it super early by playing as her in the Gestral tournament final. Other valid endgame options with the same AGI/DEF scaling are Barrier Breaker and Yeverum, the latter one in particular. Lithum is good if you plan to run her solo. She is literally so strong that you can't go wrong.

                                                            Sciel - Chation is busted from the moment you get it and changes how you play her. It's honestly a valid option all the way through, the only issue is that it being dark element (like 90% of her kit) makes her completely passive vs immune enemies. I am not a huge fan of Blizzon, since it encourages you to stay out of twilight. In the endgame, go with Litheson if you want to play her as support only, otherwise I believe Martenon is by far her strongest pick.

                                                            Verso - Dualiso, despite the low power, is unmatched until you get Cheater. After that, Corpeso allows you to mantain the same playstyle while dishing out massive damage and stacking ridicolous amounts of burn. Chevalam is strong but doesn't allow shields, starting in S is good but by that time you can reach it in just one turn with free aim into base attack spam anyway. Simoso is his best, but by the point you get it the game is over, so it only applies to NG+ onwards.

                                                            Monoco - Nusaro early on, then switch to Joyaro as soon as you get it. Ballaro/Sidaro are ok for a full support set, but I believe his potential for break and raw damage is too good to ignore.

                                                            • Anonymous
                                                              12 May 2025 21:20  

                                                              Best weapons (opinion) for each character:

                                                              Gustave: Lanceram, no effects, just a strong weapon
                                                              Maelle: Medalum, insta-Virtuoso stance. Use her to fight the secret opponent on the gestral arena to get it.
                                                              Lune: Trebuchim most of the game, Chroralim late game. Burn goes brr.
                                                              Verso: Contorso, late-endgame. U can insta-break enemies and get rank-S with the auto-death+insta revive+max break when dying+Act instantly when revived. The break with 1st bullet each turn helps too.
                                                              Lanceram is good earlier.
                                                              Sciel and Monoco... I've barely played them, but Fragaro/Bucharo/Sidaro seem good with Monoco.

                                                              Not saying anything about Sciel cause i dnt know the effs and there's a lot to read xD

                                                              • Anonymous
                                                                11 May 2025 02:13  

                                                                There are baguette weapons, you need the tokens from the start of the game and keep them for new game plus, only with them you can get baguette weapons. So it will take at least 2 new game+ runs to have all baguette weapons, if the tokens weren't spent on other stuff. The effects they have are similar to pictos and those pictos get auto removed if they were equipped. So it's more for self exploding joke builds or for those that like to have everything.
